methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Pasqal, a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by Alain Aspect and Georges-Olivier Reymond, has raised €340M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a limited peer universe: 3 publicly traded comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Traditional peer-median valuation proves inapplicable here. While 7 same-sector peers exist, none meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints when filtered by stage, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misrepresentation given the heterogeneity of quantum computing approaches and commercialization timelines across the peer set.

This analysis cannot responsibly produce a single valuation range without additional data on comparable transaction multiples, forward revenue forecasts, or cash burn dynamics specific to Pasqal's neutral-atom architecture. The capital intensity of quantum hardware development, combined with the nascency of revenue generation across the sector, limits the applicability of revenue or EBITDA multiples. A credible valuation would require either: (1) financing data from recent rounds of named peers; (2) forward-looking metrics on Pasqal's customer pipeline and deployment timeline; or (3) strategic transaction precedents in quantum hardware. Current data supports only that Pasqal operates in a capital-hungry, pre-revenue-dominant sector where peer comparability is structurally limited.
